top_signals.title: Traders Brace for Yen Swings as Holiday Intervention Risk Looms
top_signals.summary: Options traders are paying up to hedge against sharp moves in the yen ahead of thin US holiday trading, as speculation grows that Japanese authorities may be less predictable in how they intervene to support the currency.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Humanity Protocol repositions toward enterprise AI after $36 million hack, founder says
top_signals.summary: Humanity Protocol founder Terence Kwok says the project is pivoting to enterprise AI after a $36 million exploit, with fund recovery odds low.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: 4chan ridicules Ofcom again as watchdog chases unpaid £520k fine
top_signals.summary: 4chan has again mocked Ofcom after the regulator renewed demands for the controversial image generator to pay its £520,000 Online Safety Act fine, prompting its lawyer to respond with another AI-generated hamster. Preston Byrne, the US lawyer representing 4chan, posted on X on Thursday: “Ofcom wrote. Again. Demanding that 4chan pay its fine. Sent us [...]

top_signals.title: Happy birthday, America? At 250, nation may be too divided to celebrate as one
top_signals.summary: With U.S. President Trump stamping his imprint on the official commemoration, many Americans are struggling with how to separate the politics from the pageantry.
